content.problemStatement:
MoEngage noticed that some users are not aware of the practice page on CodeChef, and some others are not aware of the rated contests on CodeChef. So, MoEngage wants to send an email to the users depending on which of the following groups they fall into:



If the user has never submitted on the practice page then send an email with the text:


`https://www.codechef.com/practice`




If the user has submitted on the practice page, but never participated in a contest then send an email with the text:


`https://www.codechef.com/contests`




If the user has submitted on the practice page as well as participated in a contest then send an email with the text:


`https://discuss.codechef.com`


so that they may discuss with other members of the community.



Help MoEngage by writing a program that takes as input two integers AAA and BBB where:


A=1A = 1A=1 if the user has submitted on the practice page and 000 otherwise.
B=1B = 1B=1 if the user has participated in a contest and 000 otherwise.
Output the appropriate link to be displayed in the email.


Input Format

The input consists of a single line with two space-separated integers AAA and BBB.


Output Format

Print one of the following strings as per the problem statement.

`https://www.codechef.com/practice`
`https://www.codechef.com/contests`
`https://discuss.codechef.com`
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of users. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers A and B.
A = 1 if the user has submitted on the practice page, otherwise 0.
B = 1 if the user has participated in a contest, otherwise 0.
content.outputFormat:
For each of the T users output on a separate line one of the following URLs:
- https://www.codechef.com/practice   (if A = 0)
- https://www.codechef.com/contests   (if A = 1 and B = 0)
- https://discuss.codechef.com        (if A = 1 and B = 1)

sampleTestCases.explanation: First user: A=0 → practice page. Second user: A=0 (even though B=1) → practice page. Third user: A=1 and B=0 → contests page.
First user has both A and B equal to 1, so the discussion link is printed. Second user never submitted on practice, so the practice link is printed.

hidden.label: general case alternating
hidden.expectedOutput:
https://www.codechef.com/practice
https://www.codechef.com/contests
https://www.codechef.com/practice
https://discuss.codechef.com
https://www.codechef.com/practice
https://www.codechef.com/contests

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each case examine A first. If A is 0 the user never used the practice page, so output the practice URL. Otherwise A is 1; now check B. If B is 0 the user has not taken part in a contest, so output the contests URL. If B is also 1, output the discussion URL. This is O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T users.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
